pattern: Giving every subtree a canonical signature lets a hash map count repeats, and the first time a signature is seen twice you record one representative root.
hints: Two subtrees are equal only if they have identical shape and identical values everywhere, so encode each subtree as a string built from its structure.
A post-order serialization that includes markers for missing children makes each subtree's encoding unique to its shape and values.
Count encodings in a map; the moment a count reaches two, push that node as the single representative for that duplicate kind.

edge_inputs.description: same values but different structure are not duplicates
mirror shapes with swapped children are distinct

stress.description: random tree over a tiny value alphabet to force frequent duplicate subtrees
